messages.content:
When you try to guess the meaning of a word from the context in which the word is used, what contextis
referred to?
A. The subject and content of the text.
B. The level of complexity of the text.
C. The punctuation close to the word.
D. The text that surrounds the word.
D. The text that surrounds the word.

What is the most important part about using your own abbreviations
Group of answer choices

A. They should be easy to learn by heart

B. Others must be able to understand them

C. They should look neat and structured

D. You must be able to make sense of them
B. Others must be able to understand them

When you make summaries of court cases what should be left out?
Group of answer choices

A. All Latin term

B. Superfluous and irrelevant material

C. Law jargon

D. All content that you find confusing
B. Superfluous and irrelevant material

Long, complex sentences are typical of legal English. If you have difficulty understanding what you are reading, start by breaking up the sentence in short phrases. What will your next step be?
Group of answer choices

A. Read the sentence as many times as is necessary until the meaning is clear

B. Analyse the meaning and importance of each short phrase

C. Reconstruct the sentence to try and uncover the meaning of the sentence

D. Determine the meaning of each word that is unfamiliar to you
B. Analyse the meaning and importance of each short phrase

Which one of the following pronouns indicates possession?
Group of answer choices

A. Whom

B. Which

C. Whose

D. Where
C. Whose

Which two sections of an extract should you read with special care and concentration?
Group of answer choices

A. The heading and introductory paragraph

B. Introductory and second paragraph

C. Introductory and concluding paragraphs

D. The heading and concluding sentence
C. Introductory and concluding paragraphs

hich one of the following pronouns seems as if they represent more than one person, but they take a singular verb?
Group of answer choices

A. They

B. Their

C. Everybody

D. Themselves
C. Everybody

Why should you read the summary at the end of a textbook chapter before you start studying the actual chapter?
Group of answer choices

A. You focus your reading because you know beforehand what to expect

B. It provides you with an indication of the complexity of the chapter

C. You can decide if it is worth reading the chapter

D. It will assist you with your own summary
A. You focus your reading because you know beforehand what to expect

What must happen when sentences that contain the word ‘unless’ are rewritten as an ‘if’ sentence?
Group of answer choices

A. The type A conditional should change to a type B

B. The negative element of ‘unless’ should be brought into the ‘if’ sentence by means of the word ‘not’

C. The negative element of ‘unless’ must be brought into the ‘if’ sentence with a subtle negative

D. The ‘if’ sentence must be rewritten in the future tense
B. The negative element of ‘unless’ should be brought into the ‘if’ sentence by means of the word ‘not’

Which one of the following pronouns will correctly fill the gap in the next sentence: The appellants had to avail ______ of the opportunity to show the court the effect of squatting on their land?
Group of answer choices

A. Themselves

B. Their

C. Them

D. Itself
B. Their

Which tense is also known as the double past tense?
Group of answer choices

A. Past progressive

B. Simple past perfect

C. Past perfect

D. Simple past
C. Past perfect

Why is it not always easy to find the main idea of a text?
Group of answer choices

A. Sometimes the writer of the text fails to bring his point across

B. It may take several rereads to find the main idea if the reader is new to the topic

C. Sometimes there are several ideas of equal importance

D. Often the text is too general in its discussion to draw attention to any main idea
C. Sometimes there are several ideas of equal importance

Which one of the following tenses indicates that two actions happened in the past but that one of the actions finished earlier than the other?
Group of answer choices

A. Past progressive

B. Simple past

C. Simple past perfect

D. Past perfect
D. Past perfect

Which one of the following sentences is an example of the simple past tense?
Group of answer choices

A. The legatee died before the legacy could pass to him

B. He was trying to locate his daughter before she left for work

C. He has started drawing up his final will last week

D. After he had died his daughter inherited the business
A. The legatee died before the legacy could pass to him

As a general rule it can be said that in law text, different tenses are used in particular areas of law? Which one of the following tenses is used to describe rules and principles?
Group of answer choices

A. Present tense

B. Future tense

C. Past perfect tense

D. Present perfect tense
A. Present tense

Which one of the following requirements are recognised by our common law as a requirement for the application of the nasciturus fiction?
Group of answer choices

A. The nasciturus fiction application must be solely to the advantage of the new-born, excluding other parties

B. The nasciturus must be born in the legal-technical sense

C. The nasciturus must accrue benefits after a prescribed period following the child’s birth

D. The nasciturus must be born in a lawful documented sense
B. The nasciturus must be born in the legal-technical sense

I apologize for the confusion in my previous response. The correct answer is:

C. The nasciturus must accrue benefits after a prescribed period following the child’s birth.

Which one of the following should be your first step in answering a problem-type question?
Group of answer choices

A. Decide what tense to use

B. Define all the solutions

C. Pick out all the irrelevant facts

D. Select the essential facts
D. Select the essential facts

The law of persons is included in which one of the following aspects of law?
Group of answer choices

A. Commercial law

B. Public law

C. Conditional law

D. Private law
D. Private law

The majority of law texts include footnotes. What is the function of the footnotes?
Group of answer choices

A. To cause less disruption of the text like other means of referencing

B. To guard against unintended events of plagiarism

C. It provides comments or cross-references to other sections of a text

D. It provides pointers regarding relevant subheadings
C. It provides comments or cross-references to other sections of a text

What is the assumption of the nasciturus fiction?
Group of answer choices

A. Legal subjectivity starts at birth

B. Legal subjectivity starts at conception

C. Legal objectivity starts at birth

D. Legal observance starts at conception
B. Legal subjectivity starts at conception

Which one of the following characteristics refers to the nature of the language of statutes?
Group of answer choices

A. Sentences are mainly in the active form

B. Sentences use the second person point of view

C. Writers use short and complex incomplete sentences

D. Writers use words rarely employed in everyday language
D. Writers use words rarely employed in everyday language

Why is it often useful to quote from the text when answering questions about an extract?
Group of answer choices

A. It proves that you have read the extract

B. It substantiates your answer`

C. It is useful when you are unsure of facts

D. You can use it to link ideas
B. It substantiates your answer

‘National Health Act 61 of 3002’ is the name of an act. Why do you have to use capital letters when writing the name of the act?
Group of answer choices

A. It needs to stand out

B. It is a proper name

C. It refers to a rule

D. It is a proper noun
D. It is a proper noun

Which one of the following words are used instead of ‘will’ in an act or statute?
Group of answer choices

A. Must

B. Shall

C. Ought

D. Should
B. Shall

When you write the conclusion of your assignment you should take care to do which of the following?
Group of answer choices

A. Always start your conclusion with ‘In conclusion’

B. Refer to the assignment question and rationalise your answer

C. Refer to your topic and introductory paragraph

D. Focus on one of your points rather than including all of the
B. Refer to the assignment question and rationalise your answer

Which one of the following descriptions refers to a preamble?
Group of answer choices

A. Additional information on a constitution

B. Introductory part of a constitution or act

C. Postscript to an act or constitution

D. Supplement to an act or constitution
B. Introductory part of a constitution or act

‘Shall’ has more or less the same meaning but is much stronger than which one of the following modal verbs?
Group of answer choices

A. Can

B. Must

C. Could

D. Should
D. Should

In what way are modal verbs different from other verbs?  
Group of answer choices

A. They do no stand alone in a sentence

B. They are only used in the active voice

C. They are very seldom used in legal writing

D. They can function unaided in a sentence
A. They do not stand alone in a sentence. Modal verbs are typically used in conjunction with another verb to indicate ability, possibility, necessity, obligation, or other similar meanings.

Which one of the following sentences is the passive form of the sentence ‘They will send you to jail’?
Group of answer choices

A. You will be sent to jail

B. Jail is where you ought to be sent

C. They might be sending you to jail

D. You have been sent to jail
A. You will be sent to jail.

Which one of the following terms relate to words that represent the opposite of a particular concept or word?
Group of answer choices

A. Comparative

B. Auxiliary

C. Contravention

D. Antonym
D. Antonym

Which one of the following terms relate to words that are closely associated or similar to a particular concept or word?
Group of answer choices

A. Alternative

B. Synonym

C. Allegory

D. Simile
B. Synonym

Law students need to be aware of the power of language they use, therefore it is important to write in gender-neutral terms. Which one of the following expressions can be identified as a gender-neutral term?
Group of answer choices

A. Bachelorette

B. Chairman

C. Manpower

D. Humankind
D. Humankind

If you write an assignment in which you discuss the points of view expressed by academics in textbooks or journals or journals, you generally quote a writer in which one of the following tenses?
Group of answer choices

A. Present progressive

B. Present tense

C. Past progressive

D. Past tense
B. Present tense

Words have which of the following two types of meaning?
Group of answer choices

A. Actual and emotional

B. Dictionary and emotional

C. Original and subjective

D. Dictionary and subjectiv
B. Dictionary and emotional

Which one of the following methods can be used to simplify legal text?
Group of answer choices

A. Substitute difficult words with words that are easier to understand

B. Use fewer sentences by combining two or more sentences

C. Include detailed definitions of legal terms as part of the text

D. Use frequent repetition so that reader becomes familiar with complex concepts
A. Substitute difficult words with words that are easier to understand

Which one of the following descriptions explains the meaning of redundancy in language?
Group of answer choices

A. The accidental duplication of a section of text

B. The excessive use of words to convey something that can be said much better in a few words

C. The use of words no longer viewed as +modern English

D. The use of lengthy and unnecessary descriptions where a single word will be sufficient
B. The excessive use of words to convey something that can be said much better in a few words

Study the sentence:’…looking back in retrospect we were not allowed to enjoy, utilise and exercise our right of freedom of expression…’. Which one of the following parts of the sentence can be identified as an instance of redundancy?
Group of answer choices

A. ‘enjoy’

B. ‘looking back’

C. ‘and exercise’

D. ‘utilise’
C. 'and exercise'

Which one of the following is important to keep in mind when omitting redundant words?
Group of answer choices

A. Omit single words only and never any phrases

B. Replace redundant words with same amount of words

C. Omit words without loss of significance or meaning

D. Rephrase sentences when omitting redundant words
C. Omit words without loss of significance or meaning
